hero.subheadline: No meditation. No music. A precise external regulator for your nervous system – built on validated psychoacoustic principles.

problem.headline: The open filter
problem.body1: The neurodivergent brain has no automatic gate between stimulus and perception. Sound, light, thoughts – everything arrives simultaneously and unfiltered.
problem.body2: The result: you filter consciously. That costs enormous energy. By afternoon your mental battery is empty – not from work, but from the sheer effort of sensory processing.
problem.quote: "Instead of cognitive compensation, AuDHD-Flow provides physiological relief – bottom-up instead of top-down."

stats.text: of the population have a combined AuDHD profile. Almost no solution addresses both sides.
higher sensory processing load compared to neurotypical individuals.
neuroacoustic apps on the market combining ADHD stimulation and autism predictability in one architecture.

items.name: BASE
items.desc: Noise with the mathematical structure of natural sounds. Masks ambient noise so your brain can stop scanning the environment. Your acoustic immune system.

items.name: FOCUS
items.desc: Binaural stimulation in the attention range. Like glasses for the frontal lobe – for admin, emails, and complex tasks.

items.name: CALM
items.desc: Your brain synchronises to a calmer rhythm. Between focus and rest – ideal for creative work.

items.name: MEDITATION
items.desc: Targeted frequency stimulation for deep relaxation. Releases tension and guides the transition into quieter states of consciousness.

items.name: EVENING
items.desc: Gentle frequency stimulation for the transition to sleep. The nervous system is carefully powered down.

items.name: SAFE SPACE
items.hz: Heartbeat · Brown Noise · Vagus Nerve Anchor
items.desc: The emergency button. Brown noise combined with synchronised heartbeat and vagus nerve activation. When everything becomes too much, this mode physiologically slows your nervous system. Radical predictability – no surprises.

principles.title: Stochastic Resonance
principles.body: Moderate background noise significantly improves cognitive performance at low dopaminergic activity – by raising the signal threshold. (Sikström & Söderlund, 2007)

principles.title: Brainwave Entrainment
principles.body: The brain synchronises to rhythmic external stimuli – known as the frequency following response. EEG studies confirm that brainwaves can be guided into specific frequency ranges. (Huang & Charyton, 2008)

principles.title: Lossless Sound Generation
principles.body: The neurodivergent brain unconsciously registers compression artefacts from MP3 files as stress signals. AuDHD-Flow generates mathematically pure sine waves natively in real time – directly on the device, without compressed files.

paradox.title: The AuDHD Paradox
paradox.subtitle: Two opposing needs – one architecture.
paradox.solutionDesc: Adaptive stimulation + seamless sound loops + heartbeat synchronisation via the vagus nerve

items.title: Headphones required
items.desc: Only in-ear or noise-cancelling headphones create the closed acoustic space the binaural effect needs.

items.title: Lossless sound
items.desc: No MP3. Native real-time oscillators generate sound directly on the device. Mathematically pure sine waves – zero compression artefacts.

items.title: No tracking
items.desc: No analytics, no forced subscription, no third-party data. The app works entirely offline.

items.q: How is AuDHD-Flow different from classic meditation or focus apps?
items.a: Most apps are built for active listening – they demand your full attention for 15 or 30 minutes. AuDHD-Flow follows an uncompromising Wearable-First philosophy: it runs as a permanent acoustic companion via in-ear headphones in the background while you work or go about your day. No short-term kick – a cumulative effect. The steady, unobtrusive relief of your sensory filter means you have significantly more energy left at the end of the day. We recommend in-ear headphones with transparency mode – so you can still communicate naturally with those around you.

items.q: Does the app replace my medication (e.g. Ritalin, Elvanse)?
items.a: No. AuDHD-Flow works on the principle of physics vs. chemistry: medication intervenes chemically (top-down) in brain metabolism. The app uses physical sound waves for a bottom-up regulation of the nervous system. It perfectly complements any existing medication – but does not replace it.

items.q: Why are there so few settings and statistics in the app?
items.a: For an AuDHD brain, every additional decision layer leads to energy loss. That's why AuDHD-Flow is built on uncompromising zero-choice design: no menus, no gamification pressure. You open the app, choose your state and it runs. No decision fatigue.

items.q: How do the sounds work technically?
items.a: The audio engine uses precise, scientifically grounded frequencies, with a strict distinction between dynamic and static sounds:

bullets.title: Focus Mode
bullets.body: A gentle, dynamic wave motion stimulates the ADHD component in the brain, keeps you alert and prevents habituation. Focus is a wave.

bullets.title: All other modes (Basis, Rest, Evening, Safe Space)
bullets.body: Absolutely static, unchanging frequencies give the autistic part of the nervous system the maximum predictability and safety it needs. Safe Space is an anchor.

bullets.title: Safe Space – the emergency mode
bullets.body: When overload threatens, Safe Space combines brown noise with a precise 58 BPM heartbeat. This biological rhythm activates the vagus nerve and physiologically slows the nervous system – before a meltdown escalates.

items.q: Why not 40 Hz like on YouTube?
items.a: 40 Hz (gamma waves) can enhance performance for neurotypical brains. For an AuDHD brain, whose sensory filter is already running at full capacity, 40 Hz carries a significant risk of overload and overwhelm. AuDHD-Flow deliberately limits Focus Mode to safe beta frequencies – enough drive for focused work without overloading the nervous system.

items.q: How long should or can I use the app daily, and when will I notice an effect?
items.a: Since AuDHD-Flow works physically rather than chemically, there is no overdose and no fixed minimum usage time. The effect unfolds on two levels:

bullets.title: Immediate relief (while the app is running)
bullets.body: From the moment you switch on, the frequencies mask environmental stimuli and physically relieve your neurological sensory filter. The effect is directly noticeable – but ends when you turn the app off. Ideal for focus sprints, noisy environments, or winding down after a meeting.

bullets.title: Cumulative long-term effect (beyond active use)
bullets.body: With regular use, your nervous system begins to link the frequencies to specific states – a form of neurological conditioning. Clinical studies suggest that daily listening to specific frequencies may produce measurable neuroplastic changes after weeks. Over time, the effect increasingly carries beyond the active session.

items.a2: The build-up process typically follows three phases:

bullets2.title: Weeks 1–2 – Acoustic Shielding
bullets2.body: You notice that you're thrown off less often. Your brain's unconscious scanning activity is dampened.

bullets2.title: Weeks 2 – Month 3 – Neurological Conditioning
bullets2.body: Your nervous system links the frequencies firmly to states. The 'Mount Everest Syndrome' – where small tasks feel insurmountable – begins to ease.

bullets2.title: From Month 3 – Systemic Energy Savings
bullets2.body: By consistently using bottom-up regulation, you have significantly more capacity left in the evening – for yourself and your personal life.

items.q: Is AuDHD-Flow also useful with an ADHD-only or autism-only diagnosis?
items.a: The app was specifically designed for the AuDHD profile – people in whom ADHD and autism co-occur. The AuDHD paradox: ADHD needs stimulation, autism needs predictability. But even with a single diagnosis, the mechanisms can help:

bullets.title: For ADHD
bullets.body: Frequency-based masking (stochastic resonance) raises the stimulus threshold and improves signal processing in low-dopaminergic activity. Multiple meta-analyses confirm this effect.

bullets.title: For autism
bullets.body: The static frequencies and brown noise create a predictable acoustic environment. This reduces sensory processing load and gives the nervous system the stability it needs for self-regulation.

items.a: AuDHD-Flow was born out of personal necessity. The founder – himself diagnosed with AuDHD – was looking for a physical alternative to additional medication in his demanding professional life. Based on clinical studies on neuroacoustic effects, he built a prototype for himself. The impact in his noisy business environment was immediately noticeable: an external acoustic filter that brought order to the noise in his head. From that moment, the vision emerged to offer others living with the complex reality of AuDHD a reliable, non-pharmacological support tool. The audio engine is built on published, peer-reviewed studies – including meta-analyses with over 300 participants.
